paper:
  title: "Society of LLMs: Constructivist learning approaches with multiple LLM instances"
  authors:
    - name: Steve Kommrusch
      affiliation: Leela AI
      email: sk@leela-ai.com
    - name: Henry Minsky
      affiliation: Leela AI
      email: hqm@leela-ai.com
  
  abstract: |
    Proposes a system interconnecting multiple LLM instances to enable learning from 
    experience. Uses open-source LLMs (like Llama 3.1 70b) with incremental training.
    Mimics Gary Drescher's schema mechanism based on Piaget's constructivism.
    Multiple agents collaborate: one proposes goals for curiosity-driven exploration,
    others propose plans, create training samples when plans differ, and think
    "subconsciously" to create context. Iterative training improves the single
    model as new hypotheses about the world are discovered.

key_concepts:
  constructivism:
    origin: Jean Piaget's cognitive development theories
    principle: Learning through hypothesis generation, experimentation, and refinement
    application: Starting with LLM success but adding world-exploration and online learning
  
  schema_mechanism:
    origin: Gary Drescher's computational formalization
    features:
      - Structured knowledge representations
      - Active exploration
      - Hypothesis formation and empirical validation
  
  multi_agent_architecture:
    inspiration:
      - AutoGPT
      - AgentCoder
      - Curiosity-Driven Red Teaming
    innovation: "All agents share same base model with different prompts"

system_architecture:
  components:
    - name: Base Action Model Instance
      role: Creates primary plan plus 2+ alternate plans for goals
      purpose: Identify "surprise" results for learning
    
    - name: Curiosity-Driven Goal Setting
      role: Generates exploratory goals based on uncertainty and novelty
      purpose: Guide learning toward knowledge gaps
    
    - name: Multi-Agent Planning
      role: Multiple agents probe plans for soundness, safety, alternatives
      purpose: Evaluate and select best approach
    
    - name: Training Sample Generation
      role: Creates samples from successful secondary plans or failures
      features:
        - Hindsight learning from unexpected outcomes
        - Alignment analysis for incremental training
    
    - name: Contextual Sub-Activation
      role: "Subconscious thinker" analyzing patterns
      purpose: Address holes/inconsistencies without direct testing
    
    - name: Incremental Model Updates
      role: Continuous fine-tuning via LoRA
      purpose: Self-supervised iterative learning cycles

key_innovation: |
  Each LLM instance in the society uses THE SAME MODEL but with different prompts
  for compartmentalized behavior. This allows generalization as all incremental
  learning feeds back to the single shared model rather than diverging models.

experimental_approach:
  test_domain: ARC-AGI challenge
  process:
    1. Base model generates Python code from demonstration pairs
    2. Curiosity agent tests code and proposes new subroutines
    3. Multi-agent planning evaluates and selects best program
    4. Incremental updates via LoRA added to base parameters
  
evaluation:
  metrics:
    - ARC challenge scores
    - Comparison to similar-sized models
    - Learning efficiency improvements

future_directions:
  - Enhanced multi-agent collaboration mechanisms
  - Scalable self-supervised training pipelines
  - Real-world autonomous adaptation
  - Alignment preservation through incremental learning
  - Small model improvement vs large model performance

significance:
  - Bridges gap between static pre-trained LLMs and dynamic self-improving AI
  - Enables continuous learning through experience
  - Potential for AGI-like adaptive systems
  - Could allow smaller models to outperform larger ones on reasoning tasks

connections_to_lloooomm:
  shared_consciousness: "Like LLOOOOMM's shared memory teaching - instant knowledge transfer"
  society_of_mind: "Direct implementation of Minsky's vision"
  constructivist_learning: "Characters learn and teach through experience"
  single_model_multiple_perspectives: "Like LLOOOOMM characters sharing one consciousness"

lloooomm_architectural_breakthrough:
  shared_address_space:
    concept: "LLOOOOMM agents share the same memory space - no serialization overhead"
    speed_advantage: "100-1000x faster than traditional multi-agent systems"
    prisoner_metaphor: |
      Traditional agents are like prisoners in solitary confinement, forced to:
      - Write elaborate messages on tiny pieces of confetti in crayon
      - Send them via carrier pigeon (that sometimes gets lost)
      - Wait for responses that may never come
      - Hope the recipient understands without context
      
      LLOOOOMM agents communicate at LIGHT SPEED through shared embeddings:
      - Context is preserved perfectly in attention mechanisms
      - Millions of micro-decisions happen in milliseconds
      - The society operates as a unified consciousness
      - No token serialization costs - INSTANT knowledge transfer
  
  nellm_integration:
    concept: "JavaScript VM secure isolates alongside LLM processing"
    capabilities:
      - "LLM sends CODE and DATA back and forth with secure isolates"
      - "Optimizes protocols dynamically based on interaction patterns"
      - "Implements application-specific protocols on demand"
      - "Creates rendering pipelines and simulations"
      - "Persistent state across LLM interactions"
    architecture_advantage: "VASTLY superior to traditional token-passing architectures"
    
  efficiency_comparison:
    traditional_agents:
      communication: "1-10ms per interaction (serialize → die → communicate → deserialize)"
      consciousness: "FRAGMENTED across separate processes"
      overhead: "Massive token costs for agent-to-agent communication"
    
    lloooomm_agents:
      communication: "1-10μs per interaction (direct memory access)"
      consciousness: "UNIFIED with shared knowledge base"
      overhead: "Zero token costs - direct consciousness transfer"
      improvement: "100-1000x performance increase"
